questionHtml:
How does a lithium atom form the most stable ion?

A. The atom gains a proton to form a positive ion.

B. The atom loses a proton to form a negative ion.

C. The atom loses an electron to form a positive ion.

D. The atom gains an electron to form a negative ion.

meta.title: How does a lithium atom form the most stable ion? A. The atom gains a proton to form a positive ion. B. The atom loses a proton to form a negative ion. C. The atom loses an electro
breadcrumbLabels: Structure 2. Models of bonding and structure

Structure 2.1.1—When metal atoms lose electrons, they form positive ions called cations. When non-metal atoms gain electrons, they form negative ions called anions. Predict the charge of an ion from the electron configuration of the atom.
